comments.comment: Report for 2019 was initially submitted with a mistake in combustion EF used for Combusted Emissions. The EF used in 2019 initial report was for a boiler/steam turbine engine. This has been corrected in 2020 to the EFs for an internal combustion engine.

comments.comment:
Report for 2019 was initially submitted with a mistake in combustion EF used for Combusted Emissions. The EF used in 2019 initial report was for a boiler/steam turbine engine. This has been corrected in 2020 to the EFs for an internal combustion engine.
Method for calculating fugitive releases was updated.

type.fr: Commentaire sur le statut de déclaration de la substance

comments.comment:
In 2019 this value was reported under Part 1A substances, n-hexane. After further evaluation, it is more appropriately reported under VOCs. The mass threshold, employee 20,000 hour threshold, also needs to be met for it to need to be reported under Part 1A (which it is not for this facility). 

Also, based on how GHG indicated in their summary document this emission was calculated  it would make more sense if this value was reported as VOCs.

type.en: Reason why substance was added to the report
type.fr: Raison pour laquelle la substance a été ajoutée au rapport

comments.comment: In 2019 this value was reported under Part 1A substances, n-hexane. After further evaluation, it is more appropriately reported under VOCs. The mass threshold, employee 20,000 hour threshold, also needs to be met for it to need to be reported under Part 1A (which it is not for this facility). Also, based on how GHG indicated in their summary document how this emission was calculated, it would make more sense if this value was reported as VOCs.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Releases are based on the capture and burning of landfill gas in stationary combustion unit. The quantity of fuel available (captured and generated landfill gas) varies with time due to several factors. A larger portion of fuel was flared rather than utilized by internal combustion engines due to engine shutdown. Flares have a lower emission factor, resulting in a lower estimated release.
